lectureSummary: Mediator and promoter-proximal pausing introduce regulatory control beyond simple recruitment, linking activators and chromatin context to the transcription machinery.
keyConcepts: Mediator bridges regulatory proteins and basal machinery
Enhancers and activators influence recruitment and output indirectly
processes: Paused polymerase released into productive elongation
testableFacts: Pausing is a regulatory checkpoint rather than a failed initiation event
misconceptions: Assuming all regulation occurs only before initiation
